title: Event payload: project.agent.registered (v0.1)
description: Payload shape for project.agent.registered events. Declares an agent's identity, metadata, and capabilities in the project. run_started events reference an agent via agentRef matching this event's agentId. This event is project-state, not run-scoped.

agentId.description: Stable identifier for the agent across versions. Referenced from run_started.agentRef.

agentVersionId.description: Identifier of this specific version of the agent. MAY be omitted if the implementer does not version agents. Free-form string (semver, git hash, sequential int, ...).

name.description: Human-readable display name. Implementers SHOULD provide at least a name; an agent identified only by an opaque URI is harder to audit.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the agent does.

owner.description: Who owns / maintains the agent. Open shape. Typical fields: 'type' (team, individual, automation), 'id', 'display'.

model.description: Foundation model backing the agent. Open shape. Typical fields: 'name', 'version', 'provider'.

instructions.description: System prompt or instructions defining the agent's behavior. Open shape. Implementers SHOULD provide a hash reference, summarised form, or other redacted representation for proprietary prompts rather than inlining sensitive content.

allowedTools.description: toolIds the agent is permitted to call (static allowlist). Runtime gating via policy is a separate mechanism. MAY be omitted if the implementer gates tool access entirely at runtime.

effectiveAt.description: When this registration takes effect. MAY differ from envelope occurredAt.

supersedes.description: agentVersionId of the previous version this registration replaces.
